content:
# windows-powershell-quoting-and-paths - Overview

Automation type: windows_shell
Tooling: PowerShell, Windows, Python
Goal: Run Windows local commands with safe quoting, literal paths, and shell-aware path handling.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/microsoft.powershell.core/about/about_quoting_rules

When to use:
- Windows path contains backslashes/spaces/Chinese
- Bash ate backslashes
- PowerShell command has quotes
- Need to run Python scripts

Inputs required:
- Shell type
- Target path
- Command arguments

AI summary: On Windows, use PowerShell -NoProfile and -LiteralPath for exact paths; in Bash use forward slashes. Avoid raw backslash Windows paths in Bash and report corrected commands.

# windows-powershell-quoting-and-paths - Execution Steps

Automation type: windows_shell
Tooling: PowerShell, Windows, Python
Goal: Run Windows local commands with safe quoting, literal paths, and shell-aware path handling.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/microsoft.powershell.core/about/about_quoting_rules

- Use PowerShell -NoProfile to avoid startup hook noise when needed.
- Use -LiteralPath for exact filesystem paths.
- Use single quotes for literal PowerShell strings; double quotes when variable expansion is needed.
- In Bash/Git Bash use forward slashes such as E:/DataBase/..., not raw E:\DataBase\...
- Quote paths with spaces and avoid composing destructive commands across shells.

AI summary: On Windows, use PowerShell -NoProfile and -LiteralPath for exact paths; in Bash use forward slashes. Avoid raw backslash Windows paths in Bash and report corrected commands.

# windows-powershell-quoting-and-paths - Commands or APIs

Automation type: windows_shell
Tooling: PowerShell, Windows, Python
Goal: Run Windows local commands with safe quoting, literal paths, and shell-aware path handling.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/microsoft.powershell.core/about/about_quoting_rules

- Get-Content -LiteralPath 'E:\DataBase\...'
- python 'E:\DataBase\script.py'
- E:/DataBase/script.py in Bash
- pwsh -NoProfile -Command ...

AI summary: On Windows, use PowerShell -NoProfile and -LiteralPath for exact paths; in Bash use forward slashes. Avoid raw backslash Windows paths in Bash and report corrected commands.

# windows-powershell-quoting-and-paths - Verification

Automation type: windows_shell
Tooling: PowerShell, Windows, Python
Goal: Run Windows local commands with safe quoting, literal paths, and shell-aware path handling.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/microsoft.powershell.core/about/about_quoting_rules

- Command reads intended file
- No duplicated/corrupted path
- No conda GBK startup noise when -NoProfile is used

AI summary: On Windows, use PowerShell -NoProfile and -LiteralPath for exact paths; in Bash use forward slashes. Avoid raw backslash Windows paths in Bash and report corrected commands.

# windows-powershell-quoting-and-paths - Failure Modes

Automation type: windows_shell
Tooling: PowerShell, Windows, Python
Goal: Run Windows local commands with safe quoting, literal paths, and shell-aware path handling.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/microsoft.powershell.core/about/about_quoting_rules

- Backslashes consumed in Bash
- Quote nesting breaks command
- Chinese path encoding noise
- Wildcard expansion hits wrong files

AI summary: On Windows, use PowerShell -NoProfile and -LiteralPath for exact paths; in Bash use forward slashes. Avoid raw backslash Windows paths in Bash and report corrected commands.

# windows-powershell-quoting-and-paths - Safety Notes

Automation type: windows_shell
Tooling: PowerShell, Windows, Python
Goal: Run Windows local commands with safe quoting, literal paths, and shell-aware path handling.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/microsoft.powershell.core/about/about_quoting_rules

- Use native PowerShell file cmdlets for Windows file operations.
- Avoid destructive commands unless explicitly authorized.

AI summary: On Windows, use PowerShell -NoProfile and -LiteralPath for exact paths; in Bash use forward slashes. Avoid raw backslash Windows paths in Bash and report corrected commands.

# windows-powershell-quoting-and-paths - Handoff Report

Automation type: windows_shell
Tooling: PowerShell, Windows, Python
Goal: Run Windows local commands with safe quoting, literal paths, and shell-aware path handling.
Evidence: official / high
Source: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/microsoft.powershell.core/about/about_quoting_rules

- Shell used
- Corrected path
- Command output
- Any quoting workaround

AI summary: On Windows, use PowerShell -NoProfile and -LiteralPath for exact paths; in Bash use forward slashes. Avoid raw backslash Windows paths in Bash and report corrected commands.
